decoded.explanation: The defendant is a first-time offender who committed a non-violent financial crime and has demonstrated remorse by making partial restitution. A 10-year sentence suggested by the spectator is disproportionate to the scale of the crime and legal precedent for non-violent white-collar offenses. Taking into account the mitigating factors of a clean record and partial repayment, a sentence of 6 months serves as a punitive measure that reflects the seriousness of the breach of trust while allowing for rehabilitation.
